key_insight: Discrete-time IGO with Gaussian search distributions is shown to converge on strongly convex quadratics: the covariance collapses to zero, and the mean can reach the optimum under a bounded-condition-number condition.
engineering_relevance: This strengthens the theoretical basis for covariance-adaptive black-box optimizers like CMA-ES, especially when using fixed learning rates in continuous spaces.
method: Formulate IGO as a natural-gradient update in expectation-parameter coordinates of an exponential family.
Analyze the multivariate Gaussian family on strongly convex quadratic objectives.
Prove covariance convergence to the zero matrix and mean convergence under a bounded condition-number assumption at frequent iterations.
evidence_signal: The paper provides a theoretical convergence analysis rather than an empirical benchmark, focusing on discrete-time updates with fixed positive learning rates and quantile-based weights.
watch_item: Mean convergence is conditional on the scaled covariance matrix remaining well-conditioned often enough, so practical stability may depend on step-size and covariance dynamics.
paper_title: Beyond IGO-Flow: Toward Convergence Analysis of IGO in Continuous Spaces
